reason: Preserve Rust/Tauri ownership and dependency direction while adding security boundaries.
Apply SQLCipher, cleanup, attachment transfer, and repository contracts to file-permission hardening.
Define generated, non-sensitive diagnostics and safe command failure behavior.
Keep runtime output, telemetry, mail content, credentials, and paths out of diagnostics.
Re-audit OAuth, TLS, fixed endpoints, provider payload bounds, and credential redaction.
Integrate deterministic security, advisory, license, and workspace quality gates.
Preserve semantic Chinese UI and isolated hostile-mail rendering.
Add generated diagnostics with strict unknown-payload decoding and sensitive-field rejection.
Cover security UI, malicious corpus, lint, tests, build, and binding checks.
Trace security data from storage/provider state through IPC decoding into the UI.
Reuse URL, raster data, error, and policy validators rather than duplicating security logic.
Use the parent threat model, Tauri, SQLCipher, credential, HTML isolation, and security gate research.
